name: CBN AML/CFT/CPF Regulations
statute: CBN (Anti-Money Laundering, Combating the Financing of Terrorism and Countering Proliferation Financing of Weapons of Mass Destruction) Regulations 2022

obligations.title: Designate a Money Laundering Reporting Officer (MLRO)
obligations.description: Appoint an MLRO at management level, register the appointment with CBN, and provide the MLRO with authority and resources to discharge AML/CFT duties.

obligations.title: Board-approved AML/CFT programme
obligations.description: Maintain a written, board-approved AML/CFT programme proportionate to the institution's risk profile, reviewed at least annually.

obligations.title: Annual AML/CFT training for staff
obligations.description: Train all staff on AML/CFT obligations, red flags, and reporting duties at least annually, with targeted refreshers for customer-facing staff.

obligations.title: Screen customers and transactions against UN/NSCIA sanctions lists
obligations.description: Screen all customers at onboarding and transactions in real time against United Nations Security Council and Nigerian Sanctions Committee lists; freeze without delay on a match.

obligations.title: Retain AML/CFT records for 5 years
obligations.description: Retain customer due diligence records, transaction records, and internal SAR/STR investigation files for at least five years after the relationship ends.
